\"xpu\" %}\n    runs-on: linux.idc.xpu\n    timeout-minutes: !{{ common.timeout_minutes }}\n    !{{ upload.binary_env(config) }}\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    steps:\n      - name: Setup XPU\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/setup-xpu@release/2.10\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        id: aws_creds\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n      - name: Login to Amazon ECR\n        id: login-ecr\n        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2\n      - uses: !{{ common.download_artifact_action }}\n        name: Download Build Artifacts\n        with:\n          name: !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}\n          path: \"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/\"\n      !{{ common.checkout(deep_clone=False, directory=\"pytorch\", checkout_pr_head=False) }}\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\n          docker-image-name: !{{ config[\"container_image\"] }}\n          custom-tag-prefix: !{{ config[\"container_image_tag_prefix\"] }}\n          docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n          working-directory: pytorch\n      - name: Pull Docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      - name: Test Pytorch binary\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/test-pytorch-binary\n        env:\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      - name: Teardown XPU\n        uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-xpu\n    {%- else %}\n    runs-on: linux.rocm.gpu.mi250.1\n    timeout-minutes: !{{ common.timeout_minutes }}\n    !{{ upload.binary_env(config) }}\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    steps:\n      - name: Setup ROCm\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-rocm\n      - uses: !{{ common.download_artifact_action }}\n        name: Download Build Artifacts\n        with:\n          name: !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}\n          path: \"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/\"\n      !{{ common.checkout(deep_clone=False, directory=\"pytorch\", checkout_pr_head=False) }}\n      - name: ROCm set GPU_FLAG\n        run: |\n          echo \"GPU_FLAG=--device=/dev/mem --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video --group-add daemon\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        id: aws_creds\n        if: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\n          docker-image-name: !{{ config[\"container_image\"] }}\n          custom-tag-prefix: !{{ config[\"container_image_tag_prefix\"] }}\n          docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n          working-directory: pytorch\n      - name: Pull Docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      - name: Test Pytorch binary\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/test-pytorch-binary\n        env:\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      - name: Teardown ROCm\n        uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-rocm\n    {%- endif %}\n\n{%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n  !{{ upload.upload_binaries(config) }}\n{%- endif %}\n{% endfor %}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/templates/linux_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2"
}
45. Write @ -
{
  "content": "{% import 'common.yml.j2' as common %}\n{% import 'upload.yml.j2' as upload %}\n\n{%- block name -%}\n# Template is at:    .github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\n# Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py\nname: !{{ build_environment }}\n{%- endblock %}\n\n{%- macro set_runner_specific_vars() -%}\n      # NOTE: These environment variables are put here so that they can be applied on every job equally\n      #       They are also here because setting them at a workflow level doesn't give us access to the\n      #       runner.temp variable, which we need.\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2129\n          echo \"BINARY_ENV_FILE=${RUNNER_TEMP}/env\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2129\n          echo \"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2129\n          echo \"MAC_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n{%- endmacro %}\n\n{%- macro setup_python(py_ver) -%}\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\n        with:\n          # .4 version is min minor for 3.10, and also no-gil version of 3.13 needs at least 3.13.3\n          python-version: \"!{{ py_ver.strip('t') + ('.4' if '3.14' not in py_ver else '.0') }}\"\n          freethreaded: !{{ \"true\" if py_ver.endswith('t') else \"false\" }}\n{%- endmacro %}\n\non:\n# TODO: Migrate to new ciflow trigger, reference https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/70321\n  push:\n    # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build\n    branches:\n      - nightly\n    tags:\n      # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds\n      # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\n      - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\n{%- for label in ciflow_config.labels | sort %}\n    {%- if loop.first and branches != \"nightly\" %}\n    tags:\n    {%- endif %}\n      - '!{{ label }}/*'\n{%- endfor %}\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nenv:\n  ALPINE_IMAGE: \"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\"\n  AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\n  BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: !{{ build_environment }}\n  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n  PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n  SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 0\n!{{ common.concurrency(build_environment) }}\n\njobs:\n{%- for config in build_configs %}\n  !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}-build:\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    runs-on: !{{ macos_runner }}\n    timeout-minutes: !{{ common.timeout_minutes }}\n    !{{ upload.binary_env(config, true) }}\n    {%- if config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements is defined and config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements|d('')|length > 0  %}\n      PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: !{{ config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements }}\n    {%- endif %}\n    steps:\n      !{{ set_runner_specific_vars() }}\n      !{{ setup_python(config.get(\"python_version\", \"3.10\")) }}\n      !{{ common.checkout(deep_clone=False, directory=\"pytorch\", checkout_pr_head=False) }}\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        run: |\n          \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh\"\n      - name: Build PyTorch binary\n        run: |\n          set -eux -o pipefail\n          # shellcheck disable=SC1090\n          source \"${BINARY_ENV_FILE:-/Users/distiller/project/env}\"\n          mkdir -p \"$PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR\"\n\n          # Build\n          USE_PYTORCH_METAL_EXPORT=1\n          USE_COREML_DELEGATE=1\n          TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME=\"${TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME//-/_}\"\n          export USE_PYTORCH_METAL_EXPORT\n          export USE_COREML_DELEGATE\n          export TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME\n          \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.ci/wheel/build_wheel.sh\"\n{%- if config[\"package_type\"] == \"wheel\" %}\n      - name: Test PyTorch wheel\n        run: |\n          set -eux -o pipefail\n          # shellcheck disable=SC1090\n          source \"${BINARY_ENV_FILE:-/Users/distiller/project/env}\"\n          pip uninstall -y \"$TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME\" || true\n          pip uninstall -y \"$TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME\" || true\n\n          # Create new \"clean\" conda environment for testing\n\n          SMOKE_TEST_PARAMS=\"\"\n\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086\n          python -mvenv test_venv\n          source test_venv/bin/activate\n          pip install \"$PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR\"/*.whl numpy -v\n\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086\n          python \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.ci/pytorch/smoke_test/smoke_test.py\" --package torchonly ${SMOKE_TEST_PARAMS}\n{%- endif %}\n      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4.4.0\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          name: !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path: \"${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}\"\n  !{{ upload.upload_binaries(config, has_test=False, use_s3=False) }}\n{%- endfor %}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2"
}
46. Write @ -
{
  "content": "{% import 'common.yml.j2' as common %}\n{% import 'upload.yml.j2' as upload %}\n\n{%- block name -%}\n# Template is at:    .github/templates/windows_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\n# Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py\nname: !{{ build_environment }}\n{%- endblock %}\n\n{%- macro set_runner_specific_vars() -%}\n      # NOTE: These environment variables are put here so that they can be applied on every job equally\n      #       They are also here because setting them at a workflow level doesn't give us access to the\n      #       runner.temp variable, which we need.\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          echo \"BINARY_ENV_FILE=${RUNNER_TEMP}/env\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n          echo \"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n          echo \"WIN_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}\"\n{%- endmacro %}\n\non:\n  push:\n    {%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n    # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build\n    {%- endif %}\n    branches:\n      - !{{ branches }}\n    {%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n    tags:\n      # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds\n      # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\n      - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\n    {%- endif %}\n{%- for label in ciflow_config.labels | sort %}\n    {%- if loop.first and branches != \"nightly\" %}\n    tags:\n    {%- endif %}\n      - '!{{ label }}/*'\n{%- endfor %}\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nenv:\n  # Needed for conda builds\n  ALPINE_IMAGE: \"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\"\n  AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\n  BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: !{{ build_environment }}\n  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n  PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n  SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n  SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\n  OS: !{{ os }}\n{%- if os == \"windows-arm64\" %}\n  PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n  DOWNLOADS_DIR: c:\\temp\\downloads\n  DEPENDENCIES_DIR: c:\\temp\\dependencies\n  ENABLE_APL: 1\n  ENABLE_OPENBLAS: 0\n  MSVC_VERSION : 14.42\n{%- endif %}\n!{{ common.concurrency(build_environment) }}\n\njobs:\n  get-label-type:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n\n{%- for config in build_configs %}\n  !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}-build:\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    needs: get-label-type\n    {%- if os == \"windows-arm64\" %}\n    runs-on: \"windows-11-arm64-preview\"\n    {%- else %}\n    {%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n    runs-on: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}windows.12xlarge\"\n    {%- else %}\n    runs-on: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}windows.12xlarge.nonephemeral\"\n    {%- endif %}\n    {%- endif %}\n    timeout-minutes: !{{ common.timeout_minutes_windows_binary }}\n    !{{ upload.binary_env(config, True) }}\n    {%- if config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements is defined and config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements|d('')|length > 0  %}\n      PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: !{{ config.pytorch_extra_install_requirements }}\n    {%- endif %}\n    steps:\n{%- if os == \"windows-arm64\" %}\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          echo BINARY_ENV_FILE=%RUNNER_TEMP%/env>> %GITHUB_ENV%\n          echo PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=%RUNNER_TEMP%/artifacts>> %GITHUB_ENV%\n          echo WIN_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=%RUNNER_TEMP%>> %GITHUB_ENV%\n      - name: Bootstrap folders\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          mkdir \"%NIGHTLIES_PYTORCH_ROOT%\"\n          mkdir \"%PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR%\"\n      - name: Enable long paths\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          git config --system --get core.longpaths || echo \"core.longpaths is not set, setting it now\"\n          git config --system core.longpaths true\n      - name: Git checkout PyTorch\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          path: \"pytorch\"\n          submodules: recursive\n      - name: Bootstrap Python\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          \"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_python.bat\"\n      - name: Bootstrap APL\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          \"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_apl.bat\"\n      - name: Bootstrap Rust\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          \"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_rust.bat\"\n      - name: Bootstrap sccache\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          \"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_sccache.bat\"\n      - name: Bootstrap Libuv\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          \"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_libuv.bat\"\n{%- else %}\n      !{{ set_runner_specific_vars() }}\n      !{{ common.setup_ec2_windows() }}\n      !{{ common.checkout(deep_clone=False, directory=\"pytorch\", checkout_pr_head=False) }}\n{%- endif %}\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh\"\n      - name: Build PyTorch binary\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.circleci/scripts/binary_windows_build.sh\"\n      - uses: !{{ common.upload_artifact_action }}\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          name: !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path: \"${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}\"\n{%- if os != \"windows-arm64\" %}\n      !{{ common.wait_and_kill_ssh_windows('pytorch') }}\n{% endif %}\n  !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}-test:  # Testing\n    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    needs:\n      - !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}-build\n      - get-label-type\n{%- if os == \"windows-arm64\" %}\n    runs-on: \"windows-11-arm64-preview\"\n{%- else %}\n{%- if config[\"gpu_arch_type\"] == \"cuda\" %}\n{%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n    runs-on: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}windows.g4dn.xlarge\"\n{%- else %}\n    runs-on: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}windows.g4dn.xlarge.nonephemeral\"\n{%- endif %}\n{%- else %}\n{%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n    runs-on: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}windows.4xlarge\"\n{%- else %}\n    runs-on: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}windows.4xlarge.nonephemeral\"\n{%- endif %}\n{%- endif %}\n{%- endif %}\n    timeout-minutes: !{{ common.timeout_minutes_windows_binary }}\n    !{{ upload.binary_env(config, True) }}\n    steps:\n{%- if os == \"windows-arm64\" %}\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          echo BINARY_ENV_FILE=%RUNNER_TEMP%/env>> %GITHUB_ENV%\n          echo PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=%RUNNER_TEMP%/artifacts>> %GITHUB_ENV%\n          echo WIN_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=%RUNNER_TEMP%>> %GITHUB_ENV%\n      - name: Enable long paths\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          git config --system --get core.longpaths || echo \"core.longpaths is not set, setting it now\"\n          git config --system core.longpaths true\n      - name: Git checkout PyTorch\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          path: \"pytorch\"\n          submodules: recursive\n      - name: Bootstrap APL\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          \"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_apl.bat\"\n      - name: Bootstrap Python\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          \"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_python.bat\"\n      - name: Bootstrap Rust\n        shell: cmd\n        run: |\n          \"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_rust.bat\"\n{%- else %}\n      !{{ common.setup_ec2_windows() }}\n      !{{ common.checkout(deep_clone=False, directory=\"pytorch\", checkout_pr_head=False) }}\n      !{{ set_runner_specific_vars() }}\n{%- endif %}\n      - uses: !{{ common.download_artifact_action }}\n        name: Download Build Artifacts\n        with:\n          name: !{{ config[\"build_name\"] }}\n          path: \"${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}\"\n      - name: Populate binary env\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh\"\n      - name: Test PyTorch binary\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.circleci/scripts/binary_windows_test.sh\"\n{%- if os != \"windows-arm64\" %}\n      !{{ common.wait_and_kill_ssh_windows('pytorch') }}\n{%- endif %}\n  {%- if branches == \"nightly\" %}\n  !{{ upload.upload_binaries(config, True) }}\n  {%- endif %}\n{%- endfor %}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/templates/windows_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2"
}
47. Bash @ -
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/fetch_and_write_workflows.py << 'EOFPYTHON'\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nBatch fetch and write remaining 93 workflow files from Sourcegraph.\n\"\"\"\nimport json\nimport sys\n\n# Remaining 93 workflow files (after the 7 already written)\nworkflow_files = [\n    \".github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_docs.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_link_check.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_mac-build.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_mac-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_win-build.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_win-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/b200-symm-mem.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-manywheel-images-s390x.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-manywheel-images.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-triton-wheel.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-vllm-wheel.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/check-labels.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/close-nonexistent-disable-issues.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/create_release.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/docker-builds.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/docker-cache-rocm.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/docker-release.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/dynamo-unittest.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-linux-binary-libtorch-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-linux-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-linux-s390x-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-macos-arm64-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-macos-arm64-binary-wheel-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-windows-arm64-binary-libtorch-debug-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-windows-arm64-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-windows-arm64-binary-wheel-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-libtorch-debug-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-wheel-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/h100-cutlass-backend.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/h100-distributed.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/h100-symm-mem.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-micro-benchmark.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-compare.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-b200.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-aarch64.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-h100.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-rocm-mi300.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-rocm-mi355.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-x86-zen.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-x86.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-xpu.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-periodic.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-rocm-mi200.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-rocm-mi300.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor-unittest.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/inductor.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/lint-autoformat.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/lint-bc.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/lint.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/linux-aarch64.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/nightly-s3-uploads.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/nitpicker.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/operator_microbenchmark.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi200.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi300.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/periodic.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/pull.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/quantization-periodic.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/rocm-mi200.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/rocm-mi300.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/rocm-mi355.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/rocm-navi31.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/slow-rocm-mi200.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/slow.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/target-determination-indexer.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/target_determination.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/test-b200.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/test-check-binary.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/test-h100.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/tools-unit-tests.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/torchbench.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/trunk-rocm-mi300.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/trunk.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/unstable.yml\",\n]\n\n# Generate list of file paths for fetching\nfor i, filepath in enumerate(workflow_files, 1):\n    print(f\"{i:2d}: {filepath}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\nTotal remaining files: {len(workflow_files)}\")\nEOFPYTHON\n\npython3 /tmp/fetch_and_write_workflows.py | head -30\n",
  "description": "List remaining workflow files to process"
}
48. bash @ -
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/batch_fetch.sh << 'EOFBASH'\n#!/bin/bash\n\n# This script will generate fetch commands for all remaining workflow files\n# We'll fetch them in parallel batches of up to 3 at a time\n\nREPO=\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\"\nOUTPUT_DIR=\"/workspace\"\n\nfiles=(\n\".github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_docs.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_link_check.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_mac-build.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_mac-test.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_win-build.yml\"\n\".github/workflows/_win-test.yml\"\n)\n\necho \"#!/bin/bash\" > /tmp/fetch_batch_1.sh\necho \"# Batch 1: Files 1-15\" >> /tmp/fetch_batch_1.sh\necho \"\" >> /tmp/fetch_batch_1.sh\n\nfor file in \"${files[@]}\"; do\n    output_file=\"$OUTPUT_DIR/$file\"\n    mkdir -p \"$(dirname \"$output_file\")\"\n    echo \"# Fetching: $file\" >> /tmp/fetch_batch_1.sh\ndone\n\nchmod +x /tmp/fetch_batch_1.sh\n\necho \"Created fetch script: /tmp/fetch_batch_1.sh\"\necho \"Will fetch ${#files[@]} files in this batch\"\n\nEOFBASH\n\nchmod +x /tmp/batch_fetch.sh\nbash /tmp/batch_fetch.sh\n",
  "description": "Prepare batch fetch script"
}
49. Bash @ -
{
  "command": "ls -la /workspace/.github/workflows/ | head -20",
  "description": "Check workflow directory"
}
50. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_linux-build.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
51. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_linux-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
52. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/pull.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
53. Write @ -
{
  "content": "name: linux-build\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      docker-image-name:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Name of the base docker image to build with.\n      build-generates-artifacts:\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n        description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      cuda-arch-list:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"5.2\"\n        description: |\n          List of CUDA architectures CI build should target.\n      runner_prefix:\n        required: false\n        default: \"\"\n        type: string\n        description: Prefix for runner label\n      runner:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"linux.c7i.2xlarge\"\n        description: |\n          Label of the runner this job should run on.\n      test-matrix:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: |\n          An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This\n          is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter\n          logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds\n      selected-test-configs:\n        description: |\n          A comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep,\n          The empty list means we are going to keep every configurations by defaults\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      s3-bucket:\n        description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n      aws-role-to-assume:\n        description: Role to assume for downloading artifacts\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      disable-monitor:\n        description: |\n          Disable utilization monitoring for build job\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      monitor-log-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 5\n      monitor-data-collect-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 1\n      allow-reuse-old-whl:\n        description: |\n          If set, the build try to pull an old wheel from s3 that was built on a\n          commit with no cpp changes from this commit\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      build-additional-packages:\n        description: |\n          If set, the build job will also builds these packages and saves their\n          wheels as artifacts\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      build-external-packages:\n        description: |\n          If set, the build external packages and saves their wheels as artifacts\n          use command separated list of packages to build ex: 'vllm,transformers'.\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n\n    secrets:\n      HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n      SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\n\n    outputs:\n      docker-image:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\n        description: The docker image containing the built PyTorch.\n      test-matrix:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n        description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.\n      build-environment:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n\njobs:\n  build:\n    environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}\n    # Don't run on forked repos\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runner }}\n    timeout-minutes: 480\n    outputs:\n      docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          instructions: |\n            Build is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\n              docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\n\n      # [pytorch repo ref]\n      # Use a pytorch/pytorch reference instead of a reference to the local\n      # checkout because when we run this action we don't *have* a local\n      # checkout. In other cases you should prefer a local checkout.\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          no-sudo: true\n\n      - name: Setup Linux\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n          role-session-name: gha-linux-build\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Check if can use old whl build\n        id: use-old-whl\n        uses: ./.github/actions/reuse-old-whl\n        if: ${{ inputs.allow-reuse-old-whl }}\n        with:\n          build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          run-id: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          job-id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n\n      - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n        id: print-ghcr-mirror\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        env:\n          ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\n          echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there\n      - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)\n        id: filter\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n          selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.selected-test-configs }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Start monitoring script\n        id: monitor-script\n        if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\n          MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\n          MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\n        run: |\n          mkdir -p ../../usage_logs\n          python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7\n          python3 -m tools.stats.monitor \\\n          --log-interval \"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\" \\\n          --data-collect-interval \"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\" \\\n          > \"../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_${JOB_ID}.txt\" 2>&1 &\n          echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Download pytest cache\n        uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download\n        continue-on-error: true\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        with:\n          cache_dir: .pytest_cache\n          job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          s3_bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n      - name: Build\n        if: (steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == '') && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        id: build\n        env:\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n          SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs\n          SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\n          SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\n          XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n          PR_LABELS: ${{ toJson(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name) }}\n          TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n          XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\n          OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n          SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n          BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES: ${{ inputs.build-additional-packages }}\n          RUNNER: ${{ inputs.runner }}\n        run: |\n          START_TIME=$(date +%s)\n          if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\"s390x\"* ]]; then\n            JENKINS_USER=\n            USED_IMAGE=\"${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}\"\n            # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours\n            # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container\n            # when job is cancelled\n            DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\"sleep 12h\"\n\n            # since some steps are skipped on s390x, if they are necessary, run them here\n            env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n            env | grep '^CI' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n          else\n            JENKINS_USER=\"--user jenkins\"\n            USED_IMAGE=\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n            DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\n          fi\n\n          # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\n          TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \"%.3f \\n\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\n          # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\n          # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\n          TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\n\n          if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\"riscv64\"* ]]; then\n            # EC2 specific setup for RISC-V emulation\n            # Ensure binfmt_misc is available\n            echo \"Mounting binfmt_misc filesystem\"\n            sudo mount binfmt_misc -t binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc 2>/dev/null || true\n\n            echo \"QEMU registration: multiarch/qemu-user-static\"\n            docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes || true\n\n            # Final verification\n            echo \"Checking binfmt_misc status:\"\n            ls -la /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/ 2>/dev/null || echo \"Cannot access binfmt_misc directory\"\n\n            if [ -f /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/qemu-riscv64 ]; then\n              echo \"qemu-riscv64 registration successful\"\n            else\n              echo \"qemu-riscv64 registration failed - proceeding without emulation\"\n              echo \"This may cause RISC-V builds to fail\"\n            fi\n\n            RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=\"--privileged\"\n          else\n            RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=\n          fi\n\n          # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n          # Used for JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD, which can be empty\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            ${RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS} \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e PR_NUMBER \\\n            -e SHA1 \\\n            -e BRANCH \\\n            -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n            -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n            -e XLA_CUDA \\\n            -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n            -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n            -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \\\n            -e PR_LABELS \\\n            -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \\\n            -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n            -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n            -e BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES \\\n            -e RUNNER \\\n            --memory=\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\" \\\n            --memory-swap=\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\" \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            ${JENKINS_USER} \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${USED_IMAGE}\" \\\n            ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\\\n          )\n\n          if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\"s390x\"* ]]; then\n            docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt\"\n          fi\n\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'\n\n          END_TIME=$(date +%s)\n          echo \"build_time=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n      - name: Build external packages\n        id: build-external-packages\n        if: inputs.build-external-packages != '' &&  steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\n        uses: ./.github/actions/build-external-packages\n        with:\n          build-targets: ${{ inputs.build-external-packages }}\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n          cuda-arch-list: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}\n          output-dir: external\n\n      - name: Move external packages to dist\n        if: steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir != '' && steps.build-external-packages.outcome != 'skipped'\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          src=\"${{ steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir }}\"\n          if [ -d \"$src\" ]; then\n            mkdir -p \"dist/$(dirname \"$src\")\"\n            mv \"$src\" \"dist/$(dirname \"$src\")/\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Stop monitoring script\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        run: |\n          kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n      - name: Archive artifacts into zip\n        if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        run: |\n          zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/custom_test_artifacts build/lib build/bin .additional_ci_files\n\n      - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on S3\n        uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\n        if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path: artifacts.zip\n          s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n      - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts for s390x\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\n        if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path: artifacts.zip\n\n      - name: copy logs\n        shell: bash\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        run: |\n          rm -f ./usage_logs\n          mkdir -p ./usage_logs\n          cp ../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt ./usage_logs/\n\n      - name: Upload raw usage log to s3\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}\n        uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\n        with:\n          s3-prefix: |\n            ${{ github.repository }}/${{ github.run_id }}/${{ github.run_attempt }}/artifact\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: warn\n          path: usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt\n\n      - name: Upload sccache stats\n        if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-sccache-stats\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          build-time: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}\n\n      - name: Upload utilization stats\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\n        with:\n          job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\n          workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\n          artifact_prefix: usage_log_build_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n\n      - name: Teardown Linux\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\n        if: always() && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n\n      - name: Cleanup docker\n        if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\n          docker stop -a || true\n          docker kill -a || true\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml"
}
54. Agent @ -
{
  "description": "Continue processing remaining 90 PyTorch workflow files",
  "prompt": "You had processed files 1-10. Please continue processing files 11-100 from /tmp/files_to_update.txt. \n\nFor each remaining file:\n1. Use sg_read_file with repo: github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\n2. Apply the transformations (replace @main with @release/2.10, etc)\n3. Write to /workspace/\n\nContinue where you left off and process all remaining files. Work as efficiently as possible to complete all 100 files.",
  "resume": "a4c8d106d3e978b6d",
  "subagent_type": "general-purpose"
}
55. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
56. Write @ -
{
  "content": "# TODO: this looks sort of similar to _linux-test, but there are like a dozen\n# places where you would have to insert an if statement. Probably it's better to\n# just use a different workflow altogether\n\nname: xpu-test\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      test-matrix:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n      docker-image:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to run in.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      timeout-minutes:\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 390\n        description: |\n          Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\n      tests-to-include:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)\n      dashboard-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      disable-monitor:\n        description: |\n          [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n          Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n          since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      monitor-log-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 5\n      monitor-data-collect-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 1\n    secrets:\n      HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\nenv:\n  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\n\njobs:\n  test:\n    # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\n    strategy:\n      matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\n      fail-fast: false\n    timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    steps:\n      # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Setup XPU\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-xpu\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        id: aws_creds\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Login to Amazon ECR\n        id: login-ecr\n        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n        id: print-ghcr-mirror\n        env:\n          ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\n          echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Start monitoring script\n        id: monitor-script\n        if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\n          MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\n          MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\n        run: |\n          python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\n          python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\" --data-collect-interval \"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n          echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Download build artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n        # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\n        # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\n        # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\n        id: keep-going\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Set Test step time\n        id: test-timeout\n        shell: bash\n        env:\n          JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Test\n        id: test\n        env:\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n          GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n          GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n          SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES: 1\n          PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL: 1\n          CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n          TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n          VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n          NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n          NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n          TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n          SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n          NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n          REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n          XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n          PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n          PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n          TESTS_TO_INCLUDE: ${{ inputs.tests-to-include }}\n          DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n          HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\n        run: |\n          # Fetch aws credential from IMDs\n          eval \"$(python3 .github/scripts/get_aws_session_tokens.py)\"\n          set -x\n\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n\n          # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n          # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e PR_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n            -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n            -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n            -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n            -e JOB_ID \\\n            -e BRANCH \\\n            -e SHA1 \\\n            -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n            -e AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID \\\n            -e AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY \\\n            -e AWS_SESSION_TOKEN \\\n            -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n            -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n            -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n            -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n            -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n            -e PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES \\\n            -e PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL \\\n            -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n            -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n            -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \\\n            -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n            -e NO_TD \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n            -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n            -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \\\n            -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n            -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n            -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n            -e TESTS_TO_INCLUDE \\\n            -e ZE_AFFINITY_MASK \\\n            -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n            -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --ulimit stack=10485760:83886080 \\\n            --ulimit core=0 \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --shm-size=\"8g\" \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n            --user jenkins \\\n            --privileged \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\\\n          )\n          # save container name for later step\n          echo \"CONTAINER_NAME=${container_name}\" >> \"$GITHUB_ENV\"\n          # jenkins user does not have write permission to mounted workspace; work-around by copying within container to jenkins home\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"cd .. && cp -R workspace pytorch && cd pytorch && pip install dist/*.whl && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n      - name: Save test results\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          # copy test results back to the mounted workspace, needed sudo, resulting permissions were correct\n          docker exec -t \"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\" sh -c \"cd ../pytorch && sudo cp -R test/test-reports ../workspace/test\"\n\n      - name: Change permissions\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion }}\n        run: |\n          docker exec -t \"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\" sh -c \"sudo chown -R 1000:1000 test\"\n\n      - name: Print remaining test logs\n        shell: bash\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n        run: |\n          cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n      - name: Stop monitoring script\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        run: |\n          kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n      - name: Upload test artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n        with:\n          use-gha: true\n          file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n\n      - name: Upload utilization stats\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\n        with:\n          job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\n          workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\n\n      - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n          find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${CONTAINER_NAME}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n      - name: Stop container before exit\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          # Workaround for multiple runners on same IDC node\n          docker stop \"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\"\n\n      - name: Store Core dumps on GitHub\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\n        if: failure()\n        with:\n          name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: ignore\n          path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n      - name: Authenticate with AWS\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results\n          # The max duration enforced by the server side\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Upload the benchmark results\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\n        with:\n          benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\n          dry-run: false\n          schema-version: v3\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Teardown XPU\n        uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-xpu\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml"
}
57. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/pull.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
58. Write @ -
{
  "content": "name: pull\n\non:\n  pull_request:\n    branches-ignore:\n      - nightly\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n      - release/*\n      - landchecks/*\n    tags:\n      - ciflow/pull/*\n  workflow_dispatch:\n  schedule:\n    - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  llm-td:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  target-determination:\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n    needs: llm-td\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"docs_test\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1,  runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"jit_legacy\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"backwards_compat\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"distributed\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"distributed\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"numpy_2_x\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"libtorch_agnostic_targetting\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"openreg\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs:\n      - linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build\n      - target-determination\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-docs:\n    name: linux-docs\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_docs.yml\n    needs: linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-no-ops:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-no-ops\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-no-ops\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-pch:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-pch\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-pch\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang18-asan\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner: linux.2xlarge.memory\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang18-asan\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3-clang18-asan\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 4, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 5, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 6, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 7, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"openreg\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n      sync-tag: asan-build\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang18-asan\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs:\n      - linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build\n      - target-determination\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      sync-tag: asan-test\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12-onnx\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12-onnx\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3-clang12-onnx\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12-onnx\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs:\n      - linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build\n      - target-determination\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"crossref\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"crossref\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"dynamo_wrapped\", shard: 1, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"dynamo_wrapped\", shard: 2, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"dynamo_wrapped\", shard: 3, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"einops\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"openreg\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs:\n      - linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-build\n      - target-determination\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.14-clang12\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.14-clang12\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.14-clang12\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"crossref\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"crossref\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"dynamo_wrapped\", shard: 1, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"dynamo_wrapped\", shard: 2, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"dynamo_wrapped\", shard: 3, num_shards: 3, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"einops\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"openreg\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.14-clang12\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_8-cudnn9-py3_10-clang12-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-clang12\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-clang12\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-clang12\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cpu-py3_10-gcc11-bazel-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-cpu-py3.10-gcc11-bazel-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.large\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-bazel-test\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\n      cuda-version: cpu\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-mobile-lightweight-dispatch-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-mobile-lightweight-dispatch-build\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-mobile-lightweight-dispatch-build\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      build-generates-artifacts: false\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build:\n    # don't run build twice on main\n    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'\n    name: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3\n      sync-tag: rocm-build\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 3, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.2\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 3, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.2\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 3, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.2\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm75\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n
