78. Write @ -
{
  "content": "name: bazel\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      docker-image-name:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Name of the base docker image to build with.\n      cuda-version:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: What CUDA version to build with (i.e. \"11.7\"), \"cpu\" for none.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      test-matrix:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: |\n          A JSON description of what configs to run later on.\n      runner:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"linux.large\"\n        description: Runner type\n\nenv:\n  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\n\njobs:\n  filter:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner }}\n    outputs:\n      test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      is-test-matrix-empty: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty }}\n      keep-going: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.keep-going }}\n      reenabled-issues: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 1\n          submodules: false\n\n      - name: Select all requested test configurations\n        id: filter\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n\n  build-and-test:\n    needs: filter\n    # Don't run on forked repos.\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && needs.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False'\n    strategy:\n      matrix: ${{ fromJSON(needs.filter.outputs.test-matrix) }}\n      fail-fast: false\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n        with:\n          github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Setup Linux\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Check if in a container runner\n        shell: bash\n        id: check_container_runner\n        run: echo \"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n      - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Output disk space left\n        run: |\n          sudo df -H\n\n      - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n        run: |\n          env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n          env | grep '^CI' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Build\n        env:\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n          BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n          GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n          GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ needs.filter.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n          # TODO duplicated\n          AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\n          SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\n          SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\n          TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: 5.2\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n          OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\n        run: |\n          export SHARD_NUMBER=0\n          # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n          # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\n          # Make sure we copy test results from bazel-testlogs symlink to\n          # a regular directory ./test/test-reports\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n            -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n            -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n            -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n            -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n            -e JOB_ID \\\n            -e GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH=\"$GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH\" \\\n            -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n            -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n            -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n            -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n            -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n            -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \\\n            -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \\\n            -e CUDA_VERSION \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --shm-size=\"1g\" \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            --user jenkins \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n          )\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'\n          echo \"container_id=${container_name}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n      - name: Test\n        id: test\n        # Time out the test phase after 3.5 hours\n        timeout-minutes: 120\n        run: |\n          docker exec -t \"${container_id}\" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/test.sh && cp -Lr ./bazel-testlogs ./test/test-reports'\n\n      - name: Print remaining test logs\n        shell: bash\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n        run: |\n          cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n      - name: Chown workspace\n        uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace\n        if: always()\n\n      - name: Upload test artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n        with:\n          file-suffix: bazel-${{ github.job }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n\n      - name: Teardown Linux\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\n        if: always()\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml"
}
79. Write @ -
{
  "content": "name: linux-binary-build\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build_name:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: The build's name\n      build_environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: The build environment\n      runner_prefix:\n        required: false\n        default: \"\"\n        type: string\n        description: prefix for runner label\n      runs_on:\n        required: false\n        default: linux.12xlarge.memory.ephemeral\n        type: string\n        description: Hardware to run this \"build\" job on, linux.12xlarge or linux.arm64.2xlarge.\n      timeout-minutes:\n        required: false\n        default: 240\n        type: number\n        description: timeout for the job\n      ALPINE_IMAGE:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\"\n        description: Alpine image to use\n      PYTORCH_ROOT:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository\n      PACKAGE_TYPE:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Package type\n      DESIRED_CUDA:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Desired Cuda version\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: GPU Arch version\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: GPU Arch type\n      DOCKER_IMAGE:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to use\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image tag to use\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)\n      DESIRED_PYTHON:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired python version\n      PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Extra install requirements\n        default: \"\"\n    secrets:\n      github-token:\n        required: true\n        description: Github Token\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n\njobs:\n  build:\n    runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}\n    timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n    env:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\n      SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\n      DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\n      PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}\n      ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\n      AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\n      BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env\n      BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}\n      GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\n      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n      PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts\n      SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          {\n            echo \"PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}\"\n            echo \"PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\"\n            echo \"DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}\"\n            echo \"GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\"\n            echo \"GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\"\n            echo \"DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\"\n            echo \"SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}\"\n            echo \"LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\"\n            echo \"LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\"\n            echo \"DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\"\n            echo \"PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS=${{ env.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}\"\n            echo \"ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\"\n            echo \"AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION }}\"\n            echo \"BINARY_ENV_FILE=${{ env.BINARY_ENV_FILE }}\"\n            echo \"BUILD_ENVIRONMENT=${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}\"\n            echo \"BUILD_NAME=${{ env.BUILD_NAME }}\"\n            echo \"PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}\"\n            echo \"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}\"\n            echo \"SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}\"\n          } >> \"${GITHUB_ENV} }}\"\n\n      - name: List the env\n        shell: bash\n        run: env\n\n      - name: \"[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)\"\n        if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n        continue-on-error: true\n        with:\n          github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\n\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n\n      - name: Setup Linux\n        if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n      - name: Chown workspace\n        if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace\n        with:\n          ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\n\n      - name: Clean workspace\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          set -eux\n\n          rm -rf \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\"\n          mkdir \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\"\n\n          if [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' ]] || [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' ]] ; then\n            rm -rf \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\"\n            mkdir \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir\n        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2\n        with:\n          submodules: recursive\n          path: pytorch\n          show-progress: false\n\n      - name: Clean PyTorch checkout\n        run: |\n          # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts\n          git clean -fxd\n        working-directory: pytorch\n\n      - name: Check if the job is disabled\n        id: filter\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the\n          # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled\n          test-matrix: |\n            { include: [\n              { config: \"default\" },\n            ]}\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        id: aws_creds\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          # If doing this in release/2.10 or release branch, use docker.io. Otherwise\n          # use ECR\n          docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\n          custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}\n          # The build.sh script in this folder is not actually the correct one,\n          # this is just needed for sha calculation\n          docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n          working-directory: pytorch\n\n      - name: Pull Docker image\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Build PyTorch binary\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\n        env:\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}\n        run: |\n          set -x\n          mkdir -p artifacts/\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            -e BINARY_ENV_FILE \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e DESIRED_CUDA \\\n            -e DESIRED_PYTHON \\\n            -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n            -e GPU_ARCH_TYPE \\\n            -e GPU_ARCH_VERSION \\\n            -e LIBTORCH_VARIANT \\\n            -e PACKAGE_TYPE \\\n            -e PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR \\\n            -e PYTORCH_ROOT \\\n            -e SKIP_ALL_TESTS \\\n            -e PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/pytorch:/pytorch\" \\\n            -v \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/artifacts\" \\\n            -w / \\\n            \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n          )\n          docker exec -t -w \"${PYTORCH_ROOT}\" \"${container_name}\" bash -c \"bash .circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh\"\n          # Unified build script for all architectures (x86_64, aarch64, s390x)\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" bash -c \"source ${BINARY_ENV_FILE} && bash /pytorch/.ci/${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}/build.sh\"\n\n      - name: Chown artifacts\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          # Ensure the working directory gets chowned back to the current user\n          docker run --rm -v \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/v\" -w /v \"${ALPINE_IMAGE}\" chown -R \"$(id -u):$(id -g)\" .\n\n      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@50769540e7f4bd5e21e526ee35c689e35e0d6874 # v4.4.0\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path:\n            ${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/*\n\n      - name: Teardown Linux\n        if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Chown workspace\n        if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace\n        with:\n          ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\n\n      - name: Cleanup docker\n        if: always() && inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\n          # ignore expansion of \"docker ps -q\" since it could be empty\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2046\n          docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml"
}
80. Write @ -
{
  "content": "name: linux-binary-test\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build_name:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: The build's name\n      build_environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: The build environment\n      ALPINE_IMAGE:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\"\n      PYTORCH_ROOT:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository\n      PACKAGE_TYPE:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Package type\n      DESIRED_CUDA:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Desired Cuda version\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: GPU Arch version\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: GPU Arch type\n      DOCKER_IMAGE:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to use\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image tag to use\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)\n      DESIRED_PYTHON:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired python version\n      runner_prefix:\n        required: false\n        default: \"\"\n        type: string\n        description: prefix for runner label\n      runs_on:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Hardware to run this job on. Valid values are linux.4xlarge, linux.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu, linux.arm64.2xlarge, and linux.rocm.gpu\n    secrets:\n      github-token:\n        required: true\n        description: Github Token\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n\njobs:\n  test:\n    runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}\n    timeout-minutes: 240\n    env:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\n      SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\n      DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\n      ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\n      AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\n      BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env\n      BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}\n      GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n      PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts\n      SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          {\n            echo \"PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}\"\n            echo \"PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\"\n\n            echo \"DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}\"\n            echo \"GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\"\n            echo \"GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\"\n            echo \"DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\"\n            echo \"SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}\"\n            echo \"LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\"\n            echo \"LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\"\n            echo \"DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\"\n\n            echo \"ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\"\n            echo \"AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION }}\"\n            echo \"BINARY_ENV_FILE=${{ env.BINARY_ENV_FILE }}\"\n            echo \"BUILD_ENVIRONMENT=${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}\"\n            echo \"PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}\"\n            echo \"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}\"\n            echo \"SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}\"\n          } >> \"${GITHUB_ENV} }}\"\n\n      - name: \"[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)\"\n        if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n        continue-on-error: true\n        with:\n          github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\n\n        # Setup the environment\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n\n      - name: Setup Linux\n        if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n      - name: Chown workspace\n        if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace\n        with:\n          ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\n\n      - name: Clean workspace\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          rm -rf \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\"\n          mkdir \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\"\n\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir\n        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2\n        with:\n          submodules: recursive\n          show-progress: false\n          path: pytorch\n\n      - name: Clean PyTorch checkout\n        run: |\n          # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts\n          git clean -fxd\n        working-directory: pytorch\n\n      - name: Check if the job is disabled\n        id: filter\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the\n          # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled\n          test-matrix: |\n            { include: [\n              { config: \"default\" },\n            ]}\n\n      - name: Download Build Artifacts\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@65a9edc5881444af0b9093a5e628f2fe47ea3b2e # v4.1.7\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}\n          path: \"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/\"\n\n      - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n        id: install-nvidia-driver\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        id: aws_creds\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\n          custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}\n          docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n          working-directory: pytorch\n\n      - name: Pull Docker image\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Test Pytorch binary\n        if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/test-pytorch-binary\n        env:\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}\n\n      - name: Teardown Linux\n        if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Chown workspace\n        if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace\n        with:\n          ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml"
}
81. Write @ -
{
  "content": "name: upload\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build_name:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: The build's name\n      use_s3:\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n        description: If true, will download artifacts from s3. Otherwise will use the default GitHub artifact download action\n      PYTORCH_ROOT:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository. Not actually needed, but currently passing it in since we pass in the same inputs to the reusable workflows of all binary builds\n      PACKAGE_TYPE:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Package type\n      DESIRED_CUDA:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Desired CUDA version\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: GPU Arch version\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: GPU Arch type\n      DOCKER_IMAGE:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to use\n      DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image tag to use\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)\n      DESIRED_PYTHON:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: Desired python version\n    secrets:\n      github-token:\n        required: true\n        description: Github Token\n\njobs:\n  upload:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04\n    container:\n      image: continuumio/miniconda3:4.12.0\n    env:\n      PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\n      PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\n      # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\n      #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\n      DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}\n      GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\n      GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\n      DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\n      SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\n      LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\n      LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\n      DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\n      BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env\n      GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\n      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n      PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts\n      SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          no-sudo: true\n\n      - name: Configure AWS credentials(PyTorch account) for nightly\n        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.event.ref == 'refs/heads/nightly' }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::749337293305:role/gha_workflow_nightly_build_wheels\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Configure AWS credentials(PyTorch account) for RC builds\n        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' &&  (startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/')) }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::749337293305:role/gha_workflow_test_build_wheels\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Download Build Artifacts\n        id: download-artifacts\n        # NB: When the previous build job is skipped, there won't be any artifacts and\n        # this step will fail. Binary build jobs can only be skipped on CI, not nightly\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@65a9edc5881444af0b9093a5e628f2fe47ea3b2e # v4.1.7\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}\n          path: \"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/\"\n\n      - name: Set DRY_RUN (only for tagged pushes)\n        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.event.ref == 'refs/heads/nightly' || (startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/'))) }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"DRY_RUN=disabled\" >> \"$GITHUB_ENV\"\n\n      - name: Set UPLOAD_CHANNEL (only for tagged pushes)\n        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}\n        shell: bash -e -l {0}\n        run: |\n          # reference ends with an RC suffix\n          if [[ \"${GITHUB_REF_NAME}\" = *-rc[0-9]* ]]; then\n            echo \"UPLOAD_CHANNEL=test\" >> \"$GITHUB_ENV\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Upload binaries\n        if: steps.download-artifacts.outcome && steps.download-artifacts.outcome == 'success'\n        shell: bash\n        env:\n          PKG_DIR: \"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts\"\n          UPLOAD_SUBFOLDER: \"${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}\"\n          BUILD_NAME: ${{ inputs.build_name }}\n        run: |\n            set -ex\n            bash .circleci/scripts/binary_upload.sh\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml"
}
82. Write @ -
{
  "content": "name: build docs\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      docker-image:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to run in.\n      push:\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n        description: If set, push the docs to the docs website.\n      run-doxygen:\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n        description: If set, will enable C++ API doc generation using doxygen / breathe / exhale.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      s3-bucket:\n        description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n      aws-role-to-assume:\n        description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      upload-aws-role-to-assume:\n        description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      runner_prefix:\n        description: prefix for runner label\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n    secrets:\n      GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: Permissions for pushing to the docs site.\n\njobs:\n  build-docs:\n    # Don't run on forked repos.\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    environment: ${{ (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'pytorchbot-env' || '' }}\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        include:\n          - docs_type: cpp\n            # We recently seeing lots of exit code 137 running this in Docker indicating\n            # an OOM issue when running the job, so this upgrades the runner from 4xlarge\n            # to the next available tier of 12xlarge. So much memory just to generate cpp\n            # doc\n            runner: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix }}linux.12xlarge.memory\n            # TODO: Nightly cpp docs take longer and longer to finish (more than 3h now)\n            # Let's try to figure out how this can be improved\n            timeout-minutes: 360\n          - docs_type: python\n            runner: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\n            # It takes less than 30m to finish python docs unless there are issues\n            timeout-minutes: 30\n    # Set a fixed name for this job instead of using the current matrix-generated name, i.e. build-docs (cpp, linux.12xlarge, 180)\n    # The current name requires updating the database last docs push query from test-infra every time the matrix is updated\n    name: build-docs-${{ matrix.docs_type }}-${{ inputs.push }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n        with:\n          github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          instructions: |\n            All builds are done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\n              docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\n            To start Python docs build type:\n              cd docs && make html && make coverage\n\n      # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Setup Linux\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n          role-session-name: gha-linux-test\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Download build artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n      - name: Generate netrc (only for docs-push)\n        if: inputs.push\n        env:\n          GITHUB_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          # sometimes .netrc exists as a directory even though this is the temp folder\n          rm -rf \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\"\n          # set credentials for https pushing\n          echo \"machine github.com\" > \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\"\n          echo \"login pytorchbot\" >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\"\n          echo \"password ${GITHUB_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN}\" >> \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\"\n\n      - name: Build ${{ matrix.docs_type }} docs\n        timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.timeout-minutes }}\n        id: build-docs\n        env:\n          # After https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/88373, pull workflow can now be run periodically,\n          # so using a schedule event to determine if the docs should be pushed or not doesn't hold true\n          # anymore\n          WITH_PUSH: ${{ inputs.push }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n          DOCS_TYPE: ${{ matrix.docs_type }}\n          RUN_DOXYGEN: ${{ inputs.run-doxygen }}\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n        run: |\n          set -ex\n          # Convert refs/tags/v1.12.0rc3 into 1.12\n          if [[ \"${GITHUB_REF}\" =~ ^refs/tags/v([0-9]+\\.[0-9]+)\\\\.* ]]; then\n            target=\"${BASH_REMATCH[1]}\"\n          else\n            target=\"main\"\n          fi\n          # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e SHA1=\"$GITHUB_SHA\" \\\n            -e DOCS_VERSION=\"${target}\" \\\n            -e DOCS_TYPE \\\n            -e RUN_DOXYGEN \\\n            -e WITH_PUSH \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            --user jenkins \\\n            -v \"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\":/var/lib/jenkins/.netrc \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n          )\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" bash -c \"sudo chown -R jenkins . && pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ./.ci/pytorch/${DOCS_TYPE}_doc_push_script.sh\"\n\n      - name: Chown workspace\n        uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace\n        if: always()\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        if : ${{ inputs.upload-aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.upload-aws-role-to-assume }}\n          role-session-name: gha-linux-test\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Upload Python Docs Preview\n        uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\n        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && matrix.docs_type == 'python' && steps.build-docs.outcome == 'success' }}\n        with:\n          retention-days: 14\n          s3-bucket: doc-previews\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path: pytorch_docs/main/\n          s3-prefix: pytorch/pytorch/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n\n      - name: Upload C++ Docs Preview\n        uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\n        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && matrix.docs_type == 'cpp' && steps.build-docs.outcome == 'success' }}\n        with:\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          s3-bucket: doc-previews\n          path: cppdocs/\n          s3-prefix: pytorch/pytorch/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/cppdocs\n\n      - name: Teardown Linux\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\n        if: always()\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_docs.yml"
}
83. Write @ -
{
  "content": "on:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      runner:\n        type: string\n        required: true\n      ref:\n        type: string\n        required: true\n\njobs:\n  lint-urls:\n    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-url-lint') }}\n    uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\n    with:\n      job-name: lint-urls\n      timeout: 120\n      runner: ${{ inputs.runner }}linux.2xlarge\n      docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\n      fetch-depth: 0\n      submodules: false\n      ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}\n      script: |\n        ./scripts/lint_urls.sh $(\\\n          if [ \"${{ github.event_name }}\" = \"pull_request\" ]; then\n            echo \"${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\" \"${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\"\n          else\n            echo \"${{ github.event.before }}\" \"${{ github.sha }}\"\n          fi\n        ) || {\n          echo\n          echo \"URL lint failed.\"\n          echo \"If this is a transient outage, you can bypass it by adding the \\`skip-url-lint\\` label to your PR.\"\n          echo \"Or add \\`@lint-ignore\\` somewhere on the same line as the URL you want to skip checking.\"\n          exit 1\n        }\n\n  lint-xrefs:\n    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-xref-lint') }}\n    uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\n    with:\n      job-name: lint-xrefs\n      timeout: 60\n      runner: ${{ inputs.runner }}linux.2xlarge\n      docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\n      fetch-depth: 0\n      submodules: false\n      ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}\n      script: |\n        ./scripts/lint_xrefs.sh $(\\\n          if [ \"${{ github.event_name }}\" = \"pull_request\" ]; then\n            echo \"${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\" \"${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\"\n          else\n            echo \"${{ github.event.before }}\" \"${{ github.sha }}\"\n          fi\n        ) || {\n          echo\n          echo \"Xref lint failed.\"\n          echo \"If this is a transient outage, you can bypass it by adding the \\`skip-xref-lint\\` label to your PR.\"\n          echo \"Or add \\`@lint-ignore\\` somewhere on the same line as the reference you want to skip checking.\"\n          exit 1\n        }\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_link_check.yml"
}
